question: Can non-negative stacking convert the seven existing OOF experts' ranking complementarity into a robust default-model improvement?

stage_1_leave_one_fold_out.protocol: Fit non-negative weights, affine-logit probability scale and MCC threshold on four OOF folds; evaluate the untouched fifth fold.

stage_2_nested_calibration.protocol: Fit weights on nested train+validation roles, fit Platt and threshold only on the disjoint calibration role, and evaluate only on the outer-test role.
stage_2_nested_calibration.failed_gate: MCC did not exceed the current frozen default.

biological_and_model_interpretation.positive_evidence: All seven experts receive non-zero fold-stable weights, supporting complementary sequence-semantic and structure-aware information.
biological_and_model_interpretation.tradeoff: Dedicated calibration moves the operating point toward higher toxic-peptide recall, but the accompanying specificity loss prevents an MCC improvement.
biological_and_model_interpretation.limitation: This is training-only meta-model evidence; Test1, Test2 and ToxinPred3 were not read for this screen.

decision.paper_role: Positive ranking/reliability ablation with a decision-boundary tradeoff, not the final default model.
decision.next_action: Require a new pre-frozen external set before choosing between the current default and the stacking candidate.
